behaviors.description: Primary translator output is grammatically coherent and preserves source meaning (not literal word substitution).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in model judgment quality, not local parsing logic. REAL-INFRA: no OS/subprocess requirement. MOCK-HIDE/LLM-DEP: a stubbed translator would always hide adequacy/fluency failures; correctness depends on real NMT output and may vary across runs. MULTI-STEP: single model step, so not Workflow. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration candidate: stub API returns perfect fixture; assert coherence flag true; catches only wrapper bugs, misses real bad translations. Agentic candidate: run real NMT on a labeled bilingual set; assert adequacy/fluency threshold; catches semantic drift/literal substitution. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior text specifies one translation output quality check, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Agentic evaluation test using real translation API on a fixed bilingual benchmark; assert meaning-preservation/fluency score exceeds threshold and detect regressions where output becomes literal but semantically wrong.

behaviors.description: On translation API 503, service transparently falls back to dictionary translator and does not return an error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (API client -> fallback path -> response), not a single function. REAL-INFRA: 503 can be simulated with in-process stub; real network/OS behavior is not required by this behavior. MOCK-HIDE: mocking everything at unit level can hide wiring bugs (fallback not invoked). LLM-DEP: not dependent on model output quality, only error-path orchestration. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agentic pipeline. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Unit candidate: call fallback selector function with synthetic error object; assert selector returns fallback mode; misses integration bug where HTTP 503 is mis-mapped. Integration candidate: stub API returns 503, real fallback module + sqlite fixture; assert 200 response with fallback translation; catches broken error mapping/branching. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network needed, (b) no real AI-output correctness needed, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no LLM-governed handoff. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text is specifically about handling status code 503, which is reproducible with in-process stubs.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with stubbed primary API returning 503 and real fallback translator module/db fixture; assert caller gets successful translation response and no propagated error.

behaviors.description: Dictionary fallback returns correct pre-stored translation for phrases present in SQLite.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is DB lookup + mapping behavior across repository and translator modules. REAL-INFRA: needs local DB interaction, but not OS/network/subprocess beyond in-process sqlite. MOCK-HIDE: unit tests with mocked repo can miss SQL/key normalization/query bugs. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Unit candidate: mock repository to return known phrase; assert translator returns it; misses SQL/schema mismatch failures. Integration candidate: seed sqlite with phrase pair; call fallback translator; assert exact returned translation; catches query and mapping defects. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real external infra beyond local module+db, (b) no AI output dependency, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no LLM orchestration. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text limits scope to local SQLite phrase retrieval, which is testable in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test using seeded sqlite database and real fallback lookup path; assert known phrase returns exact stored translation.

behaviors.description: Quality tracking computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior spans metric computation plus persistence; failure appears at handoff from calculator to storage layer. REAL-INFRA: no real network/OS/subprocess needed; local in-process DB sufficient. MOCK-HIDE: pure unit tests of edit-distance function miss persistence/write-schema bugs; pure repo tests miss algorithm errors. LLM-DEP: none if fixed strings are provided. MULTI-STEP: no agentic sequence. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Unit candidate: assert editDistance('kitten','sitting')==3; catches algorithm bug but misses metric not stored. Integration candidate: inject fixed primary/fallback strings through tracker service with sqlite fixture; assert stored distance matches expected; catches compute-or-store integration failures. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text asks for correct computation/storage, not translation quality from model outputs.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with deterministic translation strings and sqlite metrics table; assert persisted edit-distance value is correct for each input pair.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, service returns primary output and logs quality comparison asynchronously.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is orchestration across primary translator, fallback translator, response selection, and async logging path. REAL-INFRA: can be tested with in-process async executor/test clock; real network/process not required if translators are stubbed. MOCK-HIDE: unit tests can miss race/ordering bugs where background log never enqueues or wrong translator is returned. LLM-DEP: assertions are about precedence and side-effect occurrence, not model output quality. MULTI-STEP: multiple components but not multiple agentic decisions. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Unit candidate: selector function picks primary when both non-null; misses async logging not triggered. Integration candidate: stub both translators, run service call with real async job path + metrics store; assert response equals primary and metric log record eventually exists; catches precedence and background-trigger defects. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required, (b) no AI-output-quality dependence, (c) no chained agentic decisions, (d) not LLM-governed handoff. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior text describes deterministic service orchestration with one async side effect, not multi-agentic reasoning stages.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with both translator dependencies available (stubbed outputs) and real async logging pipeline to local store; assert immediate response uses primary translation and quality metric is recorded asynchronously.
